filesystem.description: Read/write the local workspace tree – gives Copilot direct access to every project file without re-uploading content on each turn.

git.description: Low-level git operations (log, diff, blame, show) inside the local repo.

github.description: Full GitHub API: issues, PRs, code-search, Actions, releases, secrets across all permitted repos.

gitlab.description: GitLab API: repos, MRs, pipelines, packages.

memory.description: Persistent key-value memory that survives between Copilot sessions – stores architectural decisions, task summaries, and frequently-referenced facts to avoid re-explaining context and cut token usage significantly.

fetch.description: Lets Copilot retrieve external URLs (docs, APIs, dependency changelogs) on demand instead of relying on stale training data.

aws.description: AWS Bedrock Knowledge Base retrieval – query indexed docs and runbooks stored in S3/Bedrock without full re-upload.

cloudflare.description: Cloudflare Workers, KV, D1, R2, Pages, and AI Gateway – deploy/query Cloudflare resources directly from Copilot chat.

postgres.description: Introspect schemas and run read-only SQL queries against the Postgres database so Copilot can answer data-shape questions without manual copy-paste.

sqlite.description: Local SQLite access for development and offline testing.

docker.description: List containers, inspect images, read logs, and run docker-compose commands from Copilot.

kubernetes.description: Kubernetes cluster introspection: pods, deployments, services, config-maps.
